claims.claim: The operator of the automation claims no personal authorship credit for the writing or for the underlying experimental results beyond releasing those artifacts into the automated drafting pipeline.

evidence_refs.quote: - Checkpoint cadence: the full run prints progress every 25 tasks and writes CSV/JSON artifacts at completion; projected runtime is below the 10-minute checkpoint threshold.

"scale_limits": "The validation used GPT-2-family next-token completions, local HTTP serving, deterministic tool/state observations, and injected drift modes. It did not test live provider model changes, instruction-tuned chat models, streaming deltas, retries, rate limits, billing/API failures, long multi-tool agents, external side effects, or naturally occurring longitudinal drift.",

"scale_limits": "The validation used GPT-2-family next-token completions, local HTTP serving, deterministic tool/state observations, and injected drift modes. It did not test live provider model changes, instruction-tuned chat models, streaming deltas, retries, rate limits, billing/API failures, long multi-tool agents, external side effects, or naturally occurring longitudinal drift.",

claims.notes: Matched by lexical overlap against synced worker artifacts.

claims.claim: We report a bounded Tier 3 validation of a normalized hash-chain replay ledger for detecting and localizing drift across the request, open-weight model-fingerprint, semantic-response, tool-observation, and state components of an LLM HTTP API.

evidence_refs.quote: "claim_scope": "In a controlled local HTTP API serving cached open-weight GPT-2-family models, a normalized request/model-fingerprint/semantic-response/tool/state hash-chain replay ledger detected and localized all tested injected material drift while avoiding benign formatting false positives across 100 fixed-seed DistilGPT-2 traces.",

"claim_scope": "In a controlled local HTTP API serving cached open-weight GPT-2-family models, a normalized request/model-fingerprint/semantic-response/tool/state hash-chain replay ledger detected and localized all tested injected material drift while avoiding benign formatting false positives across 100 fixed-seed DistilGPT-2 traces.",
- Workload: local open-weight GPT-2-family next-token API served over HTTP, then replay-ledger validation with fixed prompts, drift controls, baselines, and ablations.

claims.notes: Matched by lexical overlap against synced worker artifacts.

claims.claim: The validation uses cached open-weight GPT-2-family models — `distilgpt2` as the primary model and `sshleifer/tiny-gpt2` for smoke and cross-check — served through a local `POST /v1/completions` endpoint, replacing the in-process hand-coded completion service of the prior iteration.

evidence_refs.quote: - Workload: local open-weight GPT-2-family next-token API served over HTTP, then replay-ledger validation with fixed prompts, drift controls, baselines, and ablations.

claims.notes: Matched by lexical overlap against synced worker artifacts.

claims.claim: With fixed seeds {11, 17, 23, 31, 43}, 100 prompt traces per bounded run, and 700 replay cases per validator (100 each across seven injected conditions), the full ledger (`ledger_full`) achieved 500/500 = 1.000 material drift detection, 0/200 = 0.000 benign false positives, and 500/500 = 1.000 first-component localization.

claims.claim: The output-only baseline detected only 192/500 = 0.384 of material cases and localized none; the raw-transcript baseline matched the detection rate but flagged 100/200 = 0.500 of benign formatting cases as material; component ablations lost exactly the detection or localization signal associated with the removed component.

claims.claim: Its reliability depends on whether small divergences between two runs — a reworded system prompt, a new model checkpoint, a different decoder seed, a different API serialization, or a modified tool result — can be detected, ignored when benign, and localized to the responsible component.
